leads.name: Rootstock Publishing
leads.notes: Vermont-based hybrid press founded 2014, 10-15 titles annually, Ingram distribution. Recurring multi-title print opportunity. Single-contact model directly serves hybrid publisher's author-promise requirements.

leads.name: Paul Millerd
leads.notes: Self-published author of 'The Pathless Path' (30,000+ copies sold), 30,000+ Substack subscribers, second book in development 2026. Offset pricing at 500+ copies improves per-unit economics vs KDP.

leads.name: Tortoise Books
leads.notes: Chicago-based literary press, 6-10 titles annually, 3.5 hours from Ann Arbor. Midwest peer with national distribution and growing critical reputation.

leads.name: Joanna Penn (The Creative Penn)
leads.notes: 40+ published titles, 100,000+ followers, 2026 shift to premium US-printed physical editions announced. Existing US wholesale distribution sized for 1,000+ copy offset runs.

leads.name: She Writes Press
leads.notes: Top-tier women's hybrid publisher, 35+ titles annually, consistently ranked among top North American hybrid publishers. Single-contact model matches their author-service promise.

leads.name: Restless Books
leads.notes: Translated literature press, 10-15 titles annually, strong national bookstore and library presence. Precise production specs for translated fiction benefit from single-contact manufacturing.

leads.name: Latah Books
leads.notes: Spokane indie press founded 2017, 6-10 titles annually, Ingram distribution. Growing national reach from Pacific Northwest base. Offset pricing at 500-1,000 copies outperforms current POD costs.

leads.name: CavanKerry Press
leads.notes: Fort Lee NJ poetry nonprofit, 4-6 collections annually, social justice and underrepresented voices focus. Short offset poetry runs (300-750 copies) outperform POD on quality and economics.

leads.name: Green Writers Press
leads.notes: Vermont environmental/sustainability press, 8-12 titles annually, strong New England bookstore distribution. Production choices signal editorial values -- single-contact guidance on paper and stock aligns with their mission.

leads.name: Nightboat Books
leads.notes: Brooklyn nonprofit poetry press, 8-12 titles annually, distributed through Consortium, national literary reputation. Complex typographic specifications at short-run offset volumes demand single-contact consistency.

leads.name: DoppelHouse Press
leads.notes: LA art/design/illustrated nonfiction press, 6-8 titles annually. Accurate color reproduction and image fidelity require offset over digital. Single-contact spec management from quote to press check critical for illustrated titles.

leads.name: Unsolicited Press
leads.notes: Portland indie press, 10-15 titles annually, national indie bookstore distribution. Volume pricing education (500 to 1,000 copies) helps lean press make financially sound print run decisions without in-house production expertise.

leads.name: Outpost19
leads.notes: San Francisco literary fiction press, 8-12 titles annually, strong editorial reputation. Offset at 500+ copies competitive vs POD with 20-day turnaround. Single-contact from estimate through delivery.

leads.name: Cornerstone Press
leads.notes: UW-Stevens Point student-run teaching press, 30+ titles annually, Great Lakes corridor, 6 hours from Ann Arbor. Educational manufacturing model matches teaching press mission; offset guidance extends to first-time publishers.

leads.name: Nick Gray
leads.notes: Self-published 'The 2-Hour Cocktail Party,' 10,000+ copies sold independently through website, events, and bulk corporate sales. Recurring print replenishment orders. Volume pricing education on 1,000-2,500 copy runs directly impacts his bulk sales margins.

leads.name: Kylie Lato (Midwest Foodie)
leads.notes: Minnesota food blogger/creator, 100,000+ Instagram followers, published cookbook, interested in second print run. Midwest shared identity is natural outreach hook. Single-contact guidance from quote to delivery for second run upgrade.

leads.name: Split/Lip Press
leads.notes: Chicago-area flash fiction and poetry press, 8-12 titles annually, 3.5 hours from Ann Arbor. Lean operation benefits from single-contact manufacturing with reliable 20-day turnaround.

leads.name: Santa Fe Writers Project
leads.notes: New Mexico literary press founded 2001, 8-12 titles annually, national reputation through award-winning catalog. Steady publication schedule; manufacturing consistency and single-contact model outweigh price shopping for quality-focused press.

leads.name: Stillhouse Press
leads.notes: George Mason University literary press, 3-5 titles annually, Ingram distribution. Short-run offset volumes (300-750 copies) where quality and cost-efficiency align. White-glove single-contact model adds educational value for university press editorial team.

leads.name: Wandering Aengus Press
leads.notes: Oregon micro press, 2-4 titles annually, Pacific Northwest nature writing and trail culture niche, Ingram distribution. Illustrated outdoor books require specialized paper/binding guidance. 250-copy minimum offset with single-contact spec management suits their per-title investment level.

leads.name: Catie Gett (The Staple Store Cookbook)
leads.notes: Melbourne-based cookbook creator, Kickstarter-funded 500+ copies, explicit US distribution intent. Geographic penalty (Australia) kept score low. SUPPLEMENTAL: second run for US wholesale fulfillment eliminates cross-Pacific freight for North American market -- volume pricing story directly applicable.
leads.supplemental_justification: US distribution intent makes a North American manufacturing partner viable for her second run. Cushing-Malloy printing 500-1,000 copies for direct US wholesale fulfillment eliminates cross-Pacific freight for her North American market. Direct personal email and active Kickstarter history confirm she is a real operator with print-volume intent.
